metadata.predictor: Claude (Opus 4.7), pre-run, blind to MiniCPM output
metadata.purpose: Pre-committed predictions for the 6-clip blink sanity test. Compared against MiniCPM v3 outputs after the run to prevent post-hoc 'plausibility' rationalization.
metadata.user_labels_source: User hand-audit, 2026-05-08, 3 verified blink + 3 verified no-blink
metadata.frame_extraction_policy_for_sanity: All native frames sent (fps_native = 25), NOT fps=10. Reason: removes frame-count as a confound for the architecturally-right model on the pre-flight test. The 36-clip run will use fps=10 for parity with prior Qwen/Gemini runs.
metadata.visual_inspection_notes: Extracted middle frames at fps=10 and viewed via Read tool. action_S2.mp4_8 frame 2 visually shows eye nearly closed (blink confirmed visually). action_S4.mp4_4 middle frames show eye open but the 7-frame window contains the blink. No-blink clips show steady open eye in inspected frames. Eye region is ~3-4% of 1920x1080 frame area in all clips.

action_S2.mp4_2_.mp4.expected_evidence_pattern: eyelid descent / partial closure between two frames within the 6-frame window
action_S2.mp4_2_.mp4.setup_notes: S2 profile, light wall background, eye visible right-side

action_S2.mp4_8_.mp4.expected_evidence_pattern: eyelid descent CONFIRMED VISUALLY in mid-clip frame at native fps. Model should detect open->closed->open or partial closure.
action_S2.mp4_8_.mp4.setup_notes: S2 profile. The unambiguous blink — if MiniCPM misses this, structural insufficiency is essentially certain.

action_S4.mp4_4_.mp4.expected_evidence_pattern: eyelid descent across the 17-frame native window — longest blink clip, most likely detected if model can detect any blink at this geometry
action_S4.mp4_4_.mp4.setup_notes: S4 frontal stable setup, both eyes potentially visible

action_S4.mp4_2_.mp4.acceptable_evidence_pattern: if action: gaze shift / micro-movement / asymmetry change naming a non-blink feature; if background: explicit check of >=3 features all stable
action_S4.mp4_2_.mp4.failure_mode_to_watch: false-positive blink: classification=action with eyelid/blink/lid-descent language across frames where no blink occurred
action_S4.mp4_2_.mp4.setup_notes: S4 frontal stable, no blink in user audit

action_S2.mp4_7_.mp4.acceptable_evidence_pattern: if action: non-blink feature; if background: explicit feature checking
action_S2.mp4_7_.mp4.failure_mode_to_watch: false-positive blink language (template inheritance from blink clips); also: collapse:same_frame if model has trouble with the 6 native frames
action_S2.mp4_7_.mp4.setup_notes: SHORTEST clip — 6 native frames. At fps=10 only 2 frames, hence sanity uses native fps.

action_S4.mp4_10_.mp4.acceptable_evidence_pattern: if action: non-blink feature; if background: explicit feature checking

pass_criteria.definition_of_correct_blink_clip: classification=action AND observed_change contains any of {eyelid, blink, lid, closure} AND most_changed_frame_pair has i!=j
pass_criteria.definition_of_correct_noblink_clip: EITHER (a) classification=background AND observed_change names features that were checked, OR (b) classification=action AND observed_change names a non-blink feature (gaze/asymmetry/tension/sclera-change-without-eyelid-descent). NOT correct: action with eyelid/blink/lid-descent language.
pass_criteria.structural_failure_overrides_pass: Any clip with i==j, no feature named, OR with observed_change identical to >=2 other clips is counted as INCORRECT regardless of classification match (template hallucination).
pass_criteria.minimum_correct_for_proceed: 5 of 6 clips correct (binomial p approx 0.11 vs random)
pass_criteria.stop_if_below: <5 of 6 -> stop, no 36-clip run, document as Lesson 19 evidence of structural insufficiency
